<!-- Topic 2: Arrays of Structures -->
<!-- Slides 34–71 -->

# Arrays of Structures
<!-- Slide 34 -->

## How Do We Store Many Employees? {.smaller}

+ One `Employee` variable stores one complete record.
+ What should store an entire workforce?

::: notes
Slides 34–71
:::

<!-- Slide 35 -->

---

## An Array Can Store User-Defined Types

```cpp
int scores[5];
Employee staff[5];
```

+ Every element has the same type.
+ Each `staff` element is one complete `Employee`.

<!-- Slide 36 -->

---

## Generic Syntax

```cpp
StructType arrayName[SIZE];
```

+ `StructType` determines the members in every element.
+ `SIZE` determines the fixed capacity.

<!-- Slide 37 -->

---

## Declaring an Employee Array

```cpp
const int SIZE = 5;
Employee staff[SIZE];
```

<!-- Slide 38 -->

---

## Two Operations, One Expression

```cpp
staff[i].name
```

1. `staff[i]` selects one `Employee`.
2. `.name` selects that employee’s name.

<!-- Slide 39 -->

---

## What Does This Expression Select?

```cpp
staff
staff[i]
staff[i].hourlyRate
```

+ The entire array
+ One complete record
+ One member of one record

<!-- Slide 40 -->

---

## Initializing the Entire Array

```cpp
Employee staff[] {
    {104, "Ana Ruiz", 27.50},
    {205, "Ben Lee", 31.25},
    {307, "Cam Wu", 24.75}
};
```

<!-- Slide 41 -->

---

## Default and Partial Initialization

```cpp
const int CAPACITY = 5;

Employee staff[CAPACITY] {
    {104, "Ana Ruiz", 27.50},
    {205, "Ben Lee", 31.25}
};
```

+ Remaining elements receive their member defaults.
+ `CAPACITY` counts available slots; a separate `count` tracks valid records.

<!-- Slide 42 -->

---

## Displaying Every Record

```cpp
for (int i = 0; i < SIZE; i++) {
    cout << staff[i].employeeID << "  "
         << staff[i].name << "  $"
         << staff[i].hourlyRate << '\n';
}
```

<!-- Slide 43 -->

---

## Reading Records from Input

```cpp
for (int i = 0; i < SIZE; i++) {
    cout << "Employee " << i + 1 << '\n';
    cout << "ID: ";
    cin >> staff[i].employeeID;
    cin.ignore();
    cout << "Name: ";
    getline(cin, staff[i].name);
    cout << "Rate: ";
    cin >> staff[i].hourlyRate;
}
```

<!-- Slide 44 -->

---

## Names and `getline` Inside a Record Loop

```cpp
cin >> staff[i].employeeID;
cin.ignore(1000, '\n');
getline(cin, staff[i].name);
cin >> staff[i].hourlyRate;
```

+ Remove the pending newline before reading a full name.

<!-- Slide 45 -->

---

## Passing an Array of Structures

```cpp
void printStaff(const Employee staff[], int size);
void giveRaises(Employee staff[], int size, double amount);
```

+ Pass the logical size separately.

<!-- Slide 46 -->

---

## Reading Records in a Function

```cpp
void printStaff(const Employee staff[], int size) {
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        cout << staff[i].employeeID << "  "
             << staff[i].name << "  $"
             << staff[i].hourlyRate << '\n';
    }
}
```

<!-- Slide 47 -->

---

## Modifying Records in a Function

```cpp
void giveRaises(Employee staff[], int size,
                double amount) {
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        staff[i].hourlyRate += amount;
    }
}
```

<!-- Slide 48 -->

---

## Searching by a Member

```cpp
int findEmployee(const Employee staff[], int size,
                 int targetID) {
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        if (staff[i].employeeID == targetID)
            return i;
    }
    return -1;
}
```

<!-- Slide 49 -->

---

## Returning the Matching Index

```cpp
int position = findEmployee(staff, count, 205);

if (position != -1) {
    cout << staff[position].name << '\n';
}
```

+ The index identifies the original record inside the array.

<!-- Slide 50 -->

---

## Updating One Matching Record

```cpp
int position = findEmployee(staff, count, targetID);

if (position == -1) {
    cout << "Employee not found\n";
} else {
    staff[position].hourlyRate = newRate;
}
```

<!-- Slide 51 -->

---

## Finding the Largest Member Value

```cpp
int highestPaid(const Employee staff[], int size) {
    int highest = 0;

    for (int i = 1; i < size; i++) {
        if (staff[i].hourlyRate >
            staff[highest].hourlyRate)
            highest = i;
    }
    return highest;
}
```

+ Precondition: `size > 0`.

<!-- Slide 52 -->

---

## Why Track the Index?

+ The largest rate belongs to a complete employee record.
+ The index preserves access to the employee’s ID, name, and rate.
+ The invariant: `highest` identifies the best record seen so far.

<!-- Slide 53 -->

---

## Swapping Complete Records

```cpp
Employee temp = staff[left];
staff[left] = staff[right];
staff[right] = temp;
```

+ Every member moves with its record.

<!-- Slide 54 -->

---

## Sorting by One Member

```cpp
for (int end = size - 1; end > 0; end--) {
    for (int i = 0; i < end; i++) {
        if (staff[i].hourlyRate >
            staff[i + 1].hourlyRate) {
            Employee temp = staff[i];
            staff[i] = staff[i + 1];
            staff[i + 1] = temp;
        }
    }
}
```

<!-- Slide 55 -->

---

## Sorting by Another Member

```cpp
if (staff[i].name > staff[i + 1].name) {
    Employee temp = staff[i];
    staff[i] = staff[i + 1];
    staff[i + 1] = temp;
}
```

+ The comparison chooses the ordering; the swap moves whole records.

<!-- Slide 56 -->

---

## Parallel Arrays Versus Record Arrays

```cpp
int employeeIDs[SIZE];
string names[SIZE];
double rates[SIZE];

Employee staff[SIZE];
```

+ Parallel arrays depend on a shared-index invariant.
+ A record array makes that relationship explicit.

<!-- Slide 57 -->

---

## Dynamic Arrays of Structures

```cpp
int size;
cin >> size;

Employee* staff = new Employee[size];

// Use staff[0] through staff[size - 1].

delete[] staff;
staff = nullptr;
```

<!-- Slide 58 -->

---

## Smart Ownership of a Record Array

```cpp
#include <memory>

unique_ptr<Employee[]> staff =
    make_unique<Employee[]>(size);

staff[0].name = "Ana Ruiz";
```

+ Smart ownership manages lifetime, not logical size.

<!-- Slide 59 -->

---

## Resizing Moves Complete Records

```cpp
Employee* larger = new Employee[newSize];

for (int i = 0; i < oldSize; i++)
    larger[i] = staff[i];

delete[] staff;
staff = larger;
larger = nullptr;
```

<!-- Slide 60 -->

---

## Static Array, Dynamic Array, or `vector`?

| Need | Usual Choice |
|---|---|
| Fixed compile-time capacity | Static array |
| One runtime size | Smart dynamic array |
| Automatic resizing | `vector<Employee>` |
| Manual ownership practice | Raw dynamic array |

<!-- Slide 61 -->

---

## Writing Records to a File

```cpp
void saveStaff(const Employee staff[], int size,
               const string& filename) {
    ofstream output(filename);
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        output << staff[i].employeeID << '\t'
               << staff[i].name << '\t'
               << staff[i].hourlyRate << '\n';
    }
}
```

<!-- Slide 62 -->

---

## Reading Records from a File

```cpp
int count = 0;

while (count < CAPACITY &&
       input >> staff[count].employeeID) {
    input.ignore();
    getline(input, staff[count].name, '\t');
    input >> staff[count].hourlyRate;
    count++;
}
```

<!-- Slide 63 -->

---

## Employee Directory Operations

```cpp
int findEmployee(const Employee staff[], int size,
                 int targetID);
void printStaff(const Employee staff[], int size);
bool addEmployee(Employee staff[], int& count,
                 int capacity, const Employee& worker);
bool removeEmployee(Employee staff[], int& count,
                    int targetID);
```

+ Small functions coordinate one shared record array.

<!-- Slide 64 -->

---

## Capacity Versus Logical Size

```cpp
const int CAPACITY = 100;
Employee staff[CAPACITY];
int count = 0;
```

+ `CAPACITY` is the number of available slots.
+ `count` is the number of valid records.
+ Valid records occupy indexes `0` through `count - 1`.

<!-- Slide 65 -->

---

## Appending a Record

```cpp
bool addEmployee(Employee staff[], int& count,
                 int capacity, const Employee& worker) {
    if (count >= capacity)
        return false;

    staff[count] = worker;
    count++;
    return true;
}
```

<!-- Slide 66 -->

---

## Removing a Record

```cpp
for (int i = position; i < count - 1; i++) {
    staff[i] = staff[i + 1];
}
count--;
```

+ Shifting preserves a packed valid range.

<!-- Slide 67 -->

---

## Range-Based Traversal

```cpp
for (Employee worker : staff) { }
for (Employee& worker : staff) { }
for (const Employee& worker : staff) { }
```

+ Copy, writable alias, and read-only alias are different contracts.

<!-- Slide 68 -->

---

## Pointer View of an Element

```cpp
cout << staff[i].name << '\n';
cout << (staff + i)->name << '\n';
```

+ Both expressions select the same member.
+ Subscript notation is normally clearer.

<!-- Slide 69 -->

---

## Counting Static Elements with `sizeof`

```cpp
Employee staff[5];
int size = sizeof(staff) / sizeof(staff[0]);
```

+ This works only where `staff` is still an array.
+ An array parameter has already converted to a pointer.

<!-- Slide 70 -->

---

## Arrays of Structures Summary

+ Index first to select a record; use dot notation to select its member.
+ Established search, sort, append, remove, and resize patterns work with complete records.
+ Keep capacity, logical size, and ownership responsibilities explicit.

<!-- Slide 71 -->
